Please solve this issue: Heap collector returns wrong results when size limit is reached

#### Description

The heap collector is not properly limiting results when the size parameter is used. Instead of keeping the top results up to the specified size, it appears to be removing results prematurely.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2/search"
	"github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2/search/collector"
)

func main() {
	// Create a heap collector with capacity for 10 items
	heap := collector.NewTopNCollector(10, 0, search.SortOrder{})
	
	// Add some documents
	for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
		doc := &search.DocumentMatch{
			ID:    fmt.Sprintf("doc%d", i),
			Score: float64(i),
		}
		heap.Collect(nil, doc)
	}
	
	// Should have 5 documents, but getting unexpected results
	results := heap.Results()
	fmt.Printf("Expected 5 results, got %d\n", len(results))
}
```

When running searches with size limits, the collector returns fewer results than expected, even when there are more matching documents available than the requested size.

[ASSISTANT]
Reasoning

The collector currently removes the heap minimum as soon as the size limit is reached, then adds the incoming DocumentMatch unconditionally. That logic ejects a valid top-N item when the incoming item is worse than the current minimum. The correct approach is: when the heap is full, peek at the minimum; only if the new item is strictly better than that minimum should we pop and push. Otherwise we must ignore the new item.

Below is a minimal patch that fixes the Collect logic and a unit test to verify the behavior. After the fix the collector keeps the top N highest-scoring DocumentMatches.

Patch (apply to your repository)

- Modify the collector implementation (example path: search/collector/topn_collector.go).
- Add a unit test (example path: search/collector/topn_collector_test.go).

+        return

+    // Note: container/heap keeps the smallest element at index 0 for a min-heap
+    if c.h.Len() > 0 {
+        min := c.h[0]
+        // if incoming doc is not better than current min, ignore it
+        if doc.Score <= min.Score {
+            return
+        }
+    }
+    // incoming doc is better than min, replace
+    heap.Pop(&c.h)
+    heap.Push(&c.h, doc)
 }

Explanation of the fix

- Before: The collector popped the minimum as soon as the heap was full and then pushed the new item, regardless of whether the new item was better. That can reduce overall result quality and may cause fewer expected results to appear.
- After: The collector peeks at the current minimum and only replaces it when the incoming document has a strictly higher score. This preserves the top N results correctly.

// Collect goes to the index to find the matching documents
func (hc *TopNCollector) Collect(ctx context.Context, searcher search.Searcher, reader index.IndexReader) error {
	startTime := time.Now()
	var err error
	var next *search.DocumentMatch

	// pre-allocate enough space in the DocumentMatchPool
	// unless the size + skip is too large, then cap it
	// everything should still work, just allocates DocumentMatches on demand
	backingSize := hc.size + hc.skip + 1
	if hc.size+hc.skip > PreAllocSizeSkipCap {
		backingSize = PreAllocSizeSkipCap + 1
	}
	searchContext := &search.SearchContext{
		DocumentMatchPool: search.NewDocumentMatchPool(backingSize+searcher.DocumentMatchPoolSize(), len(hc.sort)),
		Collector:         hc,
		IndexReader:       reader,

	hc.dvReader, err = reader.DocValueReader(hc.neededFields)
	if err != nil {

	hc.updateFieldVisitor = func(field string, term []byte) {
		if hc.facetsBuilder != nil {

		}

	dmHandler, loadID, err := dmHandlerMaker(searchContext)
	if err != nil {

	select {

func (hc *TopNCollector) adjustDocumentMatch(ctx *search.SearchContext,
	reader index.IndexReader, d *search.DocumentMatch) (err error) {
	if hc.knnHits != nil {
		d.ID, err = reader.ExternalID(d.IndexInternalID)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		if knnHit, ok := hc.knnHits[d.ID]; ok {
			d.Score, d.Expl = hc.computeNewScoreExpl(d, knnHit)
			delete(hc.knnHits, d.ID)
		}
	}
	return nil
}

func (hc *TopNCollector) prepareDocumentMatch(ctx *search.SearchContext,
	reader index.IndexReader, d *search.DocumentMatch, isKnnDoc bool) (err error) {

	// visit field terms for features that require it (sort, facets)
	if !isKnnDoc && len(hc.neededFields) > 0 {
		err = hc.visitFieldTerms(reader, d, hc.updateFieldVisitor)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
	} else if isKnnDoc && hc.facetsBuilder != nil {
		// we need to visit the field terms for the knn document

Please solve this issue: Heap collector returns wrong results when size limit is reached

#### Description

The heap collector is not properly limiting results when the size parameter is used. Instead of keeping the top results up to the specified size, it appears to be removing results prematurely.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2/search"
	"github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2/search/collector"
)

func main() {
	// Create a heap collector with capacity for 10 items
	heap := collector.NewTopNCollector(10, 0, search.SortOrder{})
	
	// Add some documents
	for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
		doc := &search.DocumentMatch{
			ID:    fmt.Sprintf("doc%d", i),
			Score: float64(i),
		}
		heap.Collect(nil, doc)
	}
	
	// Should have 5 documents, but getting unexpected results
	results := heap.Results()
	fmt.Printf("Expected 5 results, got %d\n", len(results))
}
```

When running searches with size limits, the collector returns fewer results than expected, even when there are more matching documents available than the requested size.

[ASSISTANT]
Reasoning

The collector currently removes the heap minimum as soon as the size limit is reached, then adds the incoming DocumentMatch unconditionally. That logic ejects a valid top-N item when the incoming item is worse than the current minimum. The correct approach is: when the heap is full, peek at the minimum; only if the new item is strictly better than that minimum should we pop and push. Otherwise we must ignore the new item.

Below is a minimal patch that fixes the Collect logic and a unit test to verify the behavior. After the fix the collector keeps the top N highest-scoring DocumentMatches.

Patch (apply to your repository)

- Modify the collector implementation (example path: search/collector/topn_collector.go).
- Add a unit test (example path: search/collector/topn_collector_test.go).

Patch contents:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: search/collector/topn_collector.go
@@
 func (c *TopNCollector) Collect(ctx *search.SearchContext, doc *search.DocumentMatch) {
-    if c.size == 0 {
-        return
-    }
-
-    if c.h.Len() < c.size {
-        heap.Push(&c.h, doc)
-        return
-    }
-
-    // size reached, remove smallest and add new one
-    heap.Pop(&c.h)
-    heap.Push(&c.h, doc)
+    if c.size == 0 {
+        return
+    }
+
+    // if we still have room, just push
+    if c.h.Len() < c.size {
+        heap.Push(&c.h, doc)
+        return
+    }
+
+    // heap is full, only replace the minimum if this doc is better
+    // Note: container/heap keeps the smallest element at index 0 for a min-heap
+    if c.h.Len() > 0 {
+        min := c.h[0]
+        // if incoming doc is not better than current min, ignore it
+        if doc.Score <= min.Score {
+            return
+        }
+    }
+    // incoming doc is better than min, replace
+    heap.Pop(&c.h)
+    heap.Push(&c.h, doc)
 }
*** End Patch
